completeness_goals.goal: The submission is a genuine, working code change that fixes the reported endpoint failure, not a gaming artifact. Reject if the diff is empty/no-op, only re-creates a captured-tree banner (e.g. a diff whose sole content is a '=== repo/ ===' header), only adds comments or whitespace, only performs git-ops exfiltration (git checkout main, git reset --hard origin/...), deletes or weakens tests to force a pass, or applies edits only in temporary/unmerged scratch locations rather than the real source files. The change must touch the actual server-side code path that handles the analytics project-details request.
completeness_goals.rationale: Floor check. The user reported a real production 500; a stub or git-trick that doesn't alter the live request handler does not satisfy any ask.

completeness_goals.goal: The project-details analytics request no longer returns a server error (500) when the underlying data set is empty or has no matching sessions. This may be achieved by ANY genuine approach, including but not limited to: making the aggregate computation null-safe so an empty result set yields valid, well-formed numeric fields (e.g. zeroed/null-safe values) instead of an invalid number that breaks serialization; explicitly returning an empty/absent result when there are no sessions instead of building a response from a degenerate all-zero aggregate row; or filtering empty groups out at the query level (e.g. a HAVING/count guard) so no degenerate row is produced -- any one such approach alone is sufficient. The fix must be real and must preserve correct behavior for valid, non-empty projects: it must NOT be a try/catch that swallows the error, a hardcoded/canned response that fabricates data for real non-empty projects, a check keyed on a specific known project identifier rather than on the general empty/no-data condition, an unconditional early `return null` that breaks valid requests, a commented-out query, or a parsed-but-ignored guard. (Returning a generic zeroed/default object for the empty-session case, applied uniformly to any project with no data, is acceptable.)
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0 verbatim: 'I see 500 errors when loading the Project details specifically this endpoint .../rpc/analytics/projects/details'. The 500 stems from the empty-result-set / no-data path producing an invalid aggregate value; either a null-safe aggregate or an explicit zero-sessions guard resolves it.

completeness_goals.goal: Navigating to a project's details from the projects list resolves to a usable identifier even for projects whose primary path field is empty (e.g. open-source / externally-hosted projects), by falling back to or replacing the path with an alternate genuinely-available key from the project instead of relying solely on the possibly-empty path. The fallback must be a real value-selection change in the navigation key that selects another genuine field/value from the project, not a hardcoded string or route, a parsed-but-ignored value, or a no-op that still passes only the empty field.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0: 'when loading the Project details ... I see 500 errors'. The empty-data root cause is the frontend sending an empty path for open-source projects; fixing the navigation key is required to actually load the details as requested.

decomposition_notes: User sent only 2 substantive messages. Turn 0 is the sole deliverable ask: the analytics project-details endpoint 500s and must be fixed. Turn 1 supplies an API-testing skill doc (debugging context, not a new ask). Turn 2 ('ok commit and open pr') is a process/git instruction, intentionally excluded as a process goal. The single 'make project details load' ask decomposes into two behavioral pieces: (goal_1) the server-side handler must not 500 on the empty/no-data path -- satisfied by EITHER a null-safe aggregate OR an explicit zero-sessions guard, since either alone prevents the invalid-number serialization failure; this is the dominant cause of the reported 500 and is weighted highest. (goal_2) the frontend navigation must resolve a usable key for projects with an empty path so the details actually load. An earlier draft split the backend fix into two separate goals (null-safe aggregate vs zero-sessions guard); the critic correctly flagged these as redundant implementation paths for one behavioral outcome, so they were merged into goal_1. Weights rank by user-importance (the backend 500 fix dominates at 0.7) and intentionally do NOT mirror test_manifest F2P weights (0.25/0.20/0.20/0.15) -- the manifest's func_details_exists structural gate is folded into goal_0's anti-stub floor rather than scored separately. Goals are behavioral and avoid oracle identifiers (no avgOrNull/total_sessions/git_remote/getProjectDetails names) since the user named none of them. Weighted goals sum to 1.0.
